summary: Polls one USAspending asynchronous download job and reports whether the export archive is ready, still processing, or failed.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint after starting a USAspending download job when the caller needs readiness, failure, elapsed time, row and column counts, and the final archive URL before attempting to fetch the file.
analyticalGrain: One response per download job status check, keyed by file_name. This is operational workflow metadata, not award-, transaction-, or account-level spending data.

primaryEntities.name: Download job
primaryEntities.description: An asynchronous backend export job identified by file_name and represented by a backend status string plus optional failure message.

primaryEntities.name: Generated archive
primaryEntities.description: The ZIP artifact referenced by file_url whose size, row count, and column count become available as the job progresses or finishes.

measures.name: Elapsed processing time
measures.description: seconds_elapsed indicates how long the job has been running or how long it took before finishing or failing.

measures.name: Archive size
measures.description: total_size reports the generated file size in kilobytes when known.

measures.name: Export row and column counts
measures.description: total_rows and total_columns summarize the generated CSV content, but they can appear before the job is fully finished.

dimensions.name: file_name
dimensions.description: The job identifier carried forward from the initiating download endpoint response.

dimensions.name: Backend job status
dimensions.description: status is a backend state label; docs list ready, running, finished, and failed, while source code shows additional possible labels such as queued, resumed, created, and uploading.

dimensions.name: Failure message
dimensions.description: message provides human-readable failure context when a job fails; it is otherwise null in observed running and finished responses.

suitableQuestions.name: Has my export finished yet?
suitableQuestions.description: Use this endpoint to poll a known file_name until the download reaches a terminal state.

suitableQuestions.name: What URL should I download once the archive is ready?
suitableQuestions.description: The response provides file_url for the generated ZIP archive.

suitableQuestions.name: How big was the export and how many rows or columns did it contain?
suitableQuestions.description: Use total_size, total_rows, and total_columns after the job reaches a stable or terminal state.

notSuitableFor.name: Exploring spending records directly
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint does not return award, transaction, or account rows; it only returns job metadata.

notSuitableFor.name: Starting a new download job
notSuitableFor.description: You must call an initiating endpoint such as a download awards, transactions, or accounts route first to obtain file_name.

notSuitableFor.name: Cataloging all available downloads
notSuitableFor.description: The endpoint looks up one exact file_name and returns 404 for unknown values rather than listing jobs.

joins.description: Award download responses return file_name and status_url that feed directly into this polling endpoint.
The same backend view is also mounted at /api/v2/bulk_download/status, so bulk download workflows share the same status semantics.

workflows.name: Start then poll a download job
workflows.description: Initiate a bounded download, capture its file_name, then poll this endpoint until the archive is ready or failed.

steps.action: Call a download initiation endpoint and retain the returned file_name or status_url.
Call v2__download__status with that exact file_name.
If status is terminal and successful, use file_url to fetch the archive; if failed, inspect message or retry the initiating endpoint.

caveats.statement: status should be treated as an open-ended backend state string, not a guaranteed four-value enum.
caveats.impact: Do not hard-fail if queued, resumed, created, or uploading appears.

caveats.statement: Error responses can contain only detail and omit the normal status payload.
caveats.impact: Branch response handling by HTTP status or by the presence of detail.

caveats.statement: Row and column counts can be provisional before the job reaches a terminal state.
caveats.impact: Do not treat nonterminal total_rows or total_columns as final analytic counts.
